name: BLACK Phantom Turbo Final
source_of_truth: ptcg_engine source + CABT official wrapper contracts
count_decisions: Drakloak is raised from two to three because its Ability is the persistent hand-quality engine and the non-Candy path to the second Dragapult.
Duskull is reduced from four to three because the bomb line is a converter, not the main attacker, and four copies overfill the Bench while competing for Rare Candy.
Dragapult remains 4-3-4 to survive Prize loss and sustain two attackers.
Dusclops and Dusknoir remain 2-2 because 50 and 130 are different Prize-routing tools; Dusclops must not always be skipped.
Energy remains Fire 6 / Psychic 8: Phantom Dive requires both colours, Psychic also activates Azelf and is the safer surplus colour.
opening_plan: Prefer going second when Cinderace is available during setup.
Use Turbo Flare only when it accelerates a concrete Dragapult or Azelf route.
Buddy-Buddy Poffin establishes Dreepy plus Duskull or Azelf without filling the Bench blindly.
Do not spend Rare Candy on the bomb line while the first Dragapult attack route is incomplete.
midgame_plan: Use Drakloak every legal turn before irreversible hand commitments when its selection can improve the route.
Complete the first Dragapult with one Fire and one Psychic, then reserve surplus Energy for the second attacker.
Phantom Dive allocates six counters one at a time toward KO, 50, 130 and future 200-damage breakpoints.
Dusclops 50 is retained for exact Prize conversion; Dusknoir 130 is used only when self-KO advances the Prize map or prevents a terminal loss.
Azelf receives Psychic Energy only when accumulated opposing damage creates a concrete one-Prize finisher route.
endgame_plan: Prime Catcher is the preferred Cinderace-to-Dragapult handoff plus gust action.
Azelf converts the opposing damage reservoir into a one-Prize lethal route.
Boss's Orders is reserved for confirmed Prize conversion.
Do not attach duplicate colours to a ready Dragapult unless Search proves immediate retreat or attack value.

effect_contracts.Drakloak: effect.id=120 card selection is scored by live route value, not raw card order.
effect_contracts.Phantom Dive: effect.id=121 chooses an opposing Bench target six times; each choice is evaluated against KO and 50/130/200 breakpoints.
effect_contracts.Dusclops: effect.id=132 selects one opposing Pokemon for five counters after self-KO.
effect_contracts.Dusknoir: effect.id=133 selects one opposing Pokemon for thirteen counters after self-KO.
effect_contracts.Crispin: effect.id=1198 selects different Basic Energy and attaches to the exact individual line missing that colour.
effect_contracts.Rare Candy: Evolve options are resolved from evolution card plus inPlay target; Dragapult attack readiness outranks nonterminal Dusknoir development.

search_package.reason: Role-specific search supports two Stage 2 lines while Drakloak supplies repeatable hand quality without discard-heavy generic search.
